item.title: Fitness Friday - Emma Hartley

item.title: I can’t draw but I consider this my art. Restored a classic Stingray in my garage.
item.description:
Day 1. She needs lots of body repair and some mechanical work.
Teaching myself fiberglass repair then applying it in small pieces around the entire body. Very satisfying work.
Most effort actually went underneath this bumper to reseat and bond the two panels before fixing fiberglass.
Brake repair. Everything had to go to remove brake lock gremlins.
Pneumatic headlight system overhaul.
Some minor interior aesthetic work to test future looks. Some people call this wasted effort, I call it fun.
Wheel strip/repaint day.
Covering up unsightly body lines.
Testing paint skills learned via YouTubeU. Priming for paint wasn’t too bad.
Primer day. Low humidity, high temp.
She’s one shade and has no body holes!!!
Post wet-sand and first coat of paint.
(Mostly) finished!
She turns heads.
A small city cruise with proper music on the radio.
item.dc:creator: iCommunicateInGifs

item.title: Art Crawl. Junior artist featured.
item.description: Not my artwork, but amazing! I only point out that she is a junior artist because more mature artists have had more time and experience to create great works. Most viral edit: if you can afford it, make a donation to your local pet shelter. If you don't have the ability, donate your time on a day off.
item.dc:creator: Mightynineftw2000

item.title: Mexican Memes for my fellow weekend mexicans
item.description:
Happy weekend. Here are memes for us mexicans, chicanos, chikonkees, gringos, and everyone else. Everyone can play along, the more the merrier.
I got el Covid so I have some days off to post memes. Currently I'm a very frozen hispanic.
All cholos welcome
I swear to god I saw my uncle do this at a party once
Horchata is kinda gross
Pt. 1
Pt. 2
The meme so nice I (accidentally) put it in twice
I'd eat it if it was non vegetarian beans with lard. And maybe some cheese
This was our high school drunk snack, but with not gross beans
Hard if taco bell, soft if homemade. Fight me.
Que lastima
Straight to jail
My family never made birria stuff, but I had some the other day and it was greasy goodness
Yum
My aunt was the queen of the chankla
I miss my Homies collection
this and heavy breathing
Infinity chankla. El Thanos es mal
OC. Years ago I met el Charmander in Denver. What a time to be alive
The bebe es hongry
Mexican, not mexican't
Pullin for all of ya. Stay well

item.title: Man just wanted some tea...
item.dc:creator: TiptronicS

item.title: Ducks.
item.description: Sauce: https://youtube.com/shorts/xZ0QDtG4usw I feel like this a lot. Not every day, but more days that not. This became a much longer write up than I had planned... TL;DR at the end. I often sit here and fantasize that someone will come along and gently but firmly pull me out of this state of "stuckness", and that I'll manage to let them, that I won't be too self conscious or embarrassed or ashamed. That I will feel so much better, and it will be a positive turning point that I will fondly look back on in years to come... But, in my 2 or 3 instances experiencing friendly interventions, since my first hospitalization back in 2017, the reality is that when friends or family do turn up, they find you when you're at your worst. You likely haven't showered for a couple (or maybe even several) days, nor brushed your hair or cleaned your teeth. You feel gross and really want to be able to tidy yourself up a bit before being forced to face the scary world that you've been avoiding so far... And so you push back, resist, try to delay or to negotiate.... But people don't have the patience for that. They say things like "but we don't care, no one cares what you smell like, we love you no matter what you look like, let's just get you out, you'll see you'll feel better almost immediately, let's just get you out". They talk to you in a singsong fake cheery voice that makes you resent them, which makes you feel like a total ingrate and undeserving of their good will. I've tried to explain (in these instances and in others) that wanting to look presentable when you have depression and anxiety isn't about vanity or fussiness - it's about dignity. And about anxiety. And depression. But for some reason the concept just doesn't stick. But I also can't blame them, they have their own lives, filled with their own issues, they don't have time to deal with trying to help someone who's bad at being helped. Or with someone who might make progress but eventually always ends up back at square 1 again and again. I can see it on their faces and hear it in their tones of voice, and in their choice of words: they're out of patience. "Why don't you just..." "But surely you can..." "You should try [insert fad/bogus/weird/impractical/unrealistic pseudo-psychology "trick" here] "Well, what keeps ME happy is..." "Oh, it sounds like you're doing better... And you've been in therapy for a while now, so when do you think you'll be properly better? Like, when will the depression be fixed?" "Yeah, but like, I think that if you didn't have such an understanding employer (who allowed you to take a block of extended unpaid mental health leave), your mental heath wouldn't be this bad, you know what I mean? Like, you would have had to just get over it or get fired, right? So like, change your mindset, just don't allow your mental health to get this bad." "What do you have to be depressed about? You have a job, a home, your health! Some people don't have that" "What do you mean you're tired? When I was your age I was out all night and working all day!" (said when I was too tired for an impromptu walk after a full day (5 hours) of therapy as an outpatient, just 1 week after a month long inpatient stay). On the other hand, I do have a couple of friends who are the absolute best and know exactly what to say and when and in which tone. It's just that they're the ones who have experienced mental health issues themselves. So I'm reluctant to let them know how bad I am, beacuse I know they'd try to help, and it makes me feel so guilty to be adding any further burden on their already heavy plates. And my mum. My mum is pretty amazing too. She's become my best ally. I honestly would never have thought that I would become close to my mum in my mid to late 30s. We weren't close when I was growing up, and I left home when I was 17 to live in a different country. But ever since I started really needing to actively deal with my metal health, she's been there (on the phone) for me. It took some educating her at first but, for someone who naturally has very little tact, she has leveled up like an absolute pro. I mean, she sometimes still says some really hurtful things when actually trying to help, but we've both learned to push through/beyond it by openly acknowledging and addressing it when it happens. Huuuge progress for both of us. But yeah, she doesn't live here (we're at lest in the same country now, but still a long distance apart), and also she has her own health issues to deal with, so I try to not burden her too much either... I do know I can't actually rely on anyone but myself to get me out of this hole... And actually, I also often fantasize that I will be my OWN hero and I will magically pull MYSELF out of it on my own - that I'll just hop up off the sofa and put on some sneakers and just go for a walk or a swim, and feel good about the world. But then I look at the dishes piling up in the sink, the dirty laundry, the unswept floors, the taxes that haven't been filed and the long list of people I haven't messaged/called/emailed/written back... And I feel guilty about leaving the house, like I don't deserve that kind of reward when there's all that work to be done... And so I just keep sitting here and doing none of it. Or, I happen to have that burst of energy/initiative at 3:00am, which is of course not a healthy time to suddenly start being a productive human being, capable of adulting. It is the actual opposite of healthy adulting. So I go to bed each day hoping that the next day will be better, that I'll magically find motivation and self-drive. That I'll become the kind of person who goes and does active things on her own... Having said that, mental health is cyclical for me. It's swing and roundabouts. There are also days where I just wake up, have a shower, and get on with the day. There aren't as many of these days as I'd like there to be (especially since COVID - omg things have definitely gotten like 1000 times worse times worse since COVID) but there ARE some, and I'm holding on to that notion like the lifeboat that it is. Phew, thanks for letting me share. I had originally wanted to just share the video with a friend, but didn't know who to send it to without sounding like I was asking to be saved... So I chose to share it with y'all fabulous people instead, which is at least better than shouting into the void. And I know it was mostly negative and totally rambling without aim or direction, but I suppose I just needed to vent. Thanks. TL;DR: mental health is tricky and can be sucky. Friends try their best to help but it's hit and miss. However I'm not giving up and I'm trying to focus on the small wins.
item.dc:creator: TeaseSpoons

item.title: Sculpture I made a while back
item.description: Reposting to be in the #artcrawl I'm thinking about making a new one today. It will be a relief sculpture. If I do, I'll probably stream while working if anyone wants to check it out. I'll keep you posted via instagram stories. I've never made a relief sculpture, nor have I ever seen anyone make one lol I'll just take a picture and imagine it as a 3D thing and let it rip. Let's see what happens. Thanks for looking! You also can find more of my work there. https://instagram.com/elbdms
item.dc:creator: elbdms

item.title: Vatican wants to know!
item.dc:creator: zacharyatticusd

item.title: My Best Friend of 15 Years Crossed The Rainbow Bridge Today
item.description:
He would have been 16 in June.
Got him from a barn back in the summer of 2006 when he was just the tiniest little thing.
Got this tattoo of him about 5 years ago, as he has been so special to me I had to show my love.
He has been there for me through a failed engagement, the darkest depressions that nearly led to suicide, and everything else...
I have thousands of pictures of him, some like this make me laugh every time I see them. Holding on to these tight right now.
He was obsessed with getting water from any possible source but his water dish, especially as a kitten.
He was the most handsome devil in the world, and he knew it. Everyone that met him fell in love, as he was so sweet and well-behaved.
As a kitten I spent so long playing with him, getting him to do back flips while chasing a feather toy, he would get so tuckered out he would fall asleep in the most awkward positions.
This was my favorite way to hold him. He would just sit in my lap and purr, never in a hurry to get down as I held him like a stuffed animal and whispered nonsense in his ear.
He always knew when I was sick, or having a particularly bad mental health day and would seek me out to comfort me.
I am feeling absolutely gutted, and could use his comfort now more than ever. The tears won't stop coming, and I feel so lost and helpless. Holding him and whispering my love into his ears as his life slipped away was easily the hardest thing I've done in my life so far. Wait, are the alerts on my phone right? Front page?? Thank you so much to everyone for sending their condolences, this is such an amazing community and I'm thankful to see so many people looking at pictures of my sweet boy and knowing how adorable he was and how much he was loved. Now my tears are more happy ❤❤
item.dc:creator: fuffycky92

item.title: Spaghetti alla puttanesca
item.description: Source: cheflorenzoboni 1 ​barillaus thick spaghetti 4​tbs evoo, divided 1​clove garlic, thinly sliced TT​red pepper flakes 2 ​tbs capers, drained [if in salt soaked and rinsed] 2-3​anchovy filets 1 ​cup of green and black olives, sliced 28​oz crushed Italian San Marzano tomatoes 1​tbs Italian parsley, chopped TT​sea salt Sauté garlic and red pepper with capers, olives and 3 tbs olive oil for several minutes over medium heat, add anchovy fillets, dissolve than add crushed tomatoes. Season with a bit of salt if needed and simmer for ten minutes. Meanwhile cook pasta nice and al dente, drain and toss with sauce. Finish with parsley and remaining olive oil before serving
item.dc:creator: 1Ofold

item.title: Goodbye, Monkey Butt
item.description: I'm sure this will be lost in user-sub. i already made a post earlier in dedication... but this is the last picture ever taken of me and my special boy earlier today. My heart is broken. You imgurians would have loved him just as much as me, he was a talker and raised to be the biggest mama's boy so he was extremely well behaved and great with young kids and other animals. I had pet rats that he played with, with his claws retracted so he wouldn't hurt them....they climbed over him and he didn't care. He was an absolute angel. He also loved flowers, broccoli, edamame, anything green - I had to put flowers on top shelves and take out trash right away with any greens in it as he would reach in and drag them out no matter how long it took... he knew when I had mental health flare ups and stuck with me however long it took. He slept with me when I was sick. He was basically an emotional support animal without the credit, and I will miss him for as long as I live
item.dc:creator: fuffycky92
